data.intent_description: BGP adjacency on R3 is down because R3 and its peers (R1, R2) lack any BGP configuration; goal is to create BGP processes and neighbor relationships so R3 forms BGP adjacencies and advertises its Loopback1 (203.0.113.1).

plan.problem: R3 has no BGP process or neighbor configuration; R1 and R2 also have no BGP configured. No BGP adjacency can form and Loopback1 (203.0.113.1) is not being advertised via BGP.
plan.fix_summary: Create a BGP process on R3, R1, and R2 using ASN 65000 (assumed). Configure neighbors using the directly connected peer IPs on Serial0/1/0 and Serial0/1/1 and advertise 203.0.113.1/32 from R3.

messages.content:
What was observed
- BGP adjacencies for R3 are down. Parsed running-configs show no BGP configuration on R1, R2 or R3. R3 has Loopback1 203.0.113.1/32 and serial links are present in the topology.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Missing BGP processes / neighbor statements on all three routers (no "router bgp" / neighbor or network statements). Additionally, attempts to set R3's serial IPs failed, so eBGP neighbor IPs on R3 are not in place.

If changes were made
- Success: configured Loopback1 on R3 (203.0.113.1/32). Configured Serial0/1/1 on R1 (10.0.0.10/30) and Serial0/1/1 on R2 (10.0.0.5/30).
- Failed: attempted to configure R3 serial interfaces (calls returned error), so R3 serial IPs were not applied.
- No BGP processes were created on any router.

Verification
- Result: FAILED. Recent get_running_config for R3 returned an error and there is no evidence BGP was configured or that 203.0.113.1 is being advertised.
- Remaining items to resolve:
  1) Successfully configure R3 serial interfaces (so neighbor IPs exist).
  2) Create BGP on R1 (e.g. AS65001), R2 (AS65002), R3 (AS65003) and add neighbor statements.
  3) Advertise 203.0.113.1 from R3 (network statement or redistribute).
  4) Re-run get_running_config and show ip bgp summary / BGP table on all routers to confirm adjacencies and advertisement.
